flags.message: Pre-1925 mandatory-jurisdiction case (Hopt v. Territory of Utah, 1880s writ of error) reached predict scope. The is_historical_mandatory exclusion needs a bare sequential docket number or a pre-1925 date_filed, but this corpus row has an empty docket_number and null dates, so it fell through; is_published_opinion_unresolvable also missed it because the row carries no citations/citation_count/opinion_text even though the docket snapshot links an opinion cluster (clusters/91057). A row-is-completely-bare-but-has-a-linked-cluster signal (or ingesting the cluster's citation data during pull) would catch this class.

Snapshot has no docket entries, no docket number, and all-null activity dates; the only substantive signal is the linked opinion cluster. The prediction is therefore driven almost entirely by era inference from the party name (Territory of Utah) and corpus base rates.
